description: Los Zetas is a fractured Mexican criminal syndicate and designated terrorist organization, known for extreme violence including beheadings and torture. Originally formed in the late 1990s as the enforcement arm of the Gulf Cartel by deserters from the Mexican Army, they broke away in 2010 to become an independent cartel. They were once Mexico's largest cartel by territory but have fragmented since the mid-2010s, with many factions absorbed or eliminated. They are based in Nuevo Laredo, Tamaulipas, and engage in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and other illegal activities.

country_links.evidence_quote: These harder drugs are shipped to the cartels through partnerships with China and other Asian countries, before being distributed out across the Americas.
country_links.context: Los Zetas receive fentanyl and other drugs shipped through partnerships with China.

country_links.context: Los Zetas teamed up with Colombian outfit Los Rastrojos and controlled drug trafficking routes in the Colombian La Guajira.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 12 June 2012, 'Z-40' and two of his brothers were arrested and indicted on charges in Texas after raids and dozens of arrests in New Mexico, Texas and Oklahoma.
country_links.context: Los Zetas have members arrested and indicted in Texas, and operate in New Mexico, Texas, and Oklahoma; they also transport drugs and humans across borders into the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Early in 2012 it was reported that Los Zetas were operating in the northern Venezuela – Colombia border
country_links.context: Los Zetas were operating in the northern Venezuela–Colombia border and controlled drug trafficking routes in the Venezuelan state of Zulia.
